Notes: Measure with bore gauge at 3 points (near reed end; midpoint; near bell)
Measure at 3 points. Taper OK within ±0.010" over length.
Record actual length after tuning trim. This IS the calibrated value.
Measure from tip to shank base before fitting
Measure with electronic tuner (Korg OT-120 preferred). Tune reed bridle before measuring. Record temperature + RH.
Record cents deviation from equal temperament. Traditional playing uses just intonation — deviation expected and acceptable.

Target: No squeaking or instability at mf dynamic
Notes: Adjust bridle until stable oscillation. Record bridle position description.

Target: Overblows to 3rd harmonic (a twelfth) at full pressure
Notes: Duduks typically don't overblow cleanly — limited range is characteristic. Note if any upper mode is accessible.
Check after 48-hr cure. Apply 3rd coat if still absorbing.

Target: No runs or bare patches visible

Notes: Has the cultural context section been reviewed by someone with knowledge of Armenian music? Recommend: yes before making the repo public.

Target: No hiss audible around reed socket
Notes: Check with reed seated and playing. Adjust PTFE wrap if leaking.
Record: tuner model, temperature (°F), relative humidity (%), player

Target: Korg OT-120 or equivalent chromatic tuner
